hints.Gutta: 'Gutta' is a noun — the subject of the sentence
hints.lapidem: 'lapidem' is a noun — the direct object
hints.cadendo: 'cadendo' is a gerund (verbal noun), not the main verb

hints.to be careful: 'to be careful' is 'cavere' — 'cavat' comes from 'cavare' (to hollow out)

hints.caveo, cavere, cavi, cautum: These are the principal parts of 'cavere' (to beware)
hints.cado, cadere, cecidi, casum: These are the principal parts of 'cadere' (to fall)
hints.capio, capere, cepi, captum: These are the principal parts of 'capere' (to seize)

hints.Ablative: Ablative of 'gutta' would be 'gutta' — but here it is the subject, so nominative
hints.Vocative: Vocative would look like 'gutta' too, but context shows it is the subject — nominative

hints.2nd: 2nd declension nouns end in -us/-um — 'gutta' with -a ending is 1st declension
hints.3rd: 3rd declension has varied endings — 'gutta' is a typical 1st declension -a noun
hints.4th: 4th declension nouns end in -us/-u — 'gutta' is 1st declension

hints.Nominative: Nominative of 'lapis' is 'lapis' — 'lapidem' with -em ending is accusative

hints.1st: 1st declension nouns end in -a — 'lapis' with genitive 'lapidis' is 3rd declension
hints.2nd: 2nd declension nouns end in -us/-um — 'lapis' is 3rd declension
hints.5th: 5th declension nouns end in -es — 'lapis' has genitive 'lapidis', which is 3rd declension

hints.Nominative: Nominative of 'vis' is 'vis' — 'vi' is the ablative singular
hints.Dative: 'vis' is irregular and lacks a standard dative singular — 'vi' is ablative

hints.Present participle: Present participle would be 'cadens/cadentis' — 'cadendo' with -ndo is a gerund
hints.Gerundive: Gerundive would be 'cadendus/a/um' — 'cadendo' is the ablative of the gerund
hints.Perfect participle: Perfect participle of 'cadere' would be 'casus' — 'cadendo' is a gerund

translation: A drop hollows out a stone not by force but by falling often
